etymology.description: semantic 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") + phonetic 爻(爻) (yáo, "marks") -> mixed
etymology.interpretation: The character 駁 (bó, "mixed") uses the semantic component 馬(馬) (mǎ, "horse") combined with the phonetic component 爻(爻) (yáo, "marks"), suggesting the idea of a horse with mixed or mottled markings, leading to the general sense of something being mixed or having varied characteristics.

examples.chinese: 他的论点很难被驳倒(他的論點很難被駁倒)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de lùndiǎn hěn nán bèi bódǎo.
examples.english: His argument is difficult to refute.

examples.chinese: 我们正在讨论这个驳杂的主题(我們正在討論這個駁雜的主題)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zhèngzài tǎolùn zhège bózá de zhǔtí.
examples.english: We are discussing this mixed topic.

examples.chinese: 艺术家的作品充满了驳杂的色彩(藝術家的作品充滿了駁雜的色彩)。
examples.pinyin: Yìshùjiā de zuòpǐn chōngmǎnle bówé de sècǎi.
examples.english: The artist's work is full of mixed colors.
